ItacPersonItem.Notes: Portrait from Giulio Cesare Capaccio, Epistolarum, 1615. For other portraits of Giambattista Marino, see Marino, Lettere, 1628; Crasso, Elogij, Venice, 1666. Marino Elected Principe of the Umoristi in 1623. His portrait as Umorista is taken from Strage de gli Innocenti. ... con la risposta.-Discorso Accademico. - Vita del Cavalier G. B. Marino descritta dal Cavaliere F. Ferrari, etc.), and come originally from a portrait by J. F. Greuter, after a painting by Simon Vouet (see Crelly, Giovanetti (1989), figure 6)
ItacPersonItem.Citation: Lorenza Gianfrancesco and Simone Testa, directed by Jane Everson, Denis Reidy, Lisa Sampson, assisted by Thomas Denman, for the Italian Academies Database project.
